title: Handling events
description: onClick, onChange, and the synthetic event.

questions.explanation: Different from HTML — onClick, not onclick.

questions.prompt: Which fires the handler on render (bug)?
options: On every render — say is CALLED immediately
questions.explanation: Wrap in an arrow: onClick={() => say('hi')} — or pass the reference.
return false doesn't cancel React events.

options: Cross-browser normalized wrapper; e.nativeEvent gives you the raw event
questions.explanation: You almost never need nativeEvent — the wrapper mirrors it.

questions.prompt: Which is called when you type into the input?
questions.explanation: React's onChange behaves like native input event — fires per keystroke.
Wrap in arrow. Otherwise you call the fn during render.
